title: senecio xerophilus
intro: Senecio xerophilus, also known as the dryland ragwort, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Senecio xerophilus include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Senecio xerophilus can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or oil to create a tea or tincture that can be used to treat various health conditions.
